museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: November 2005: Xbox 360 launches in North America on November 22.
museum.curatorNote: Initial researched draft for November 2005. Regional timing and UK retail context should be checked before verification.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: Guitar Hero
items.body: A plastic guitar turns living rooms into awkward, joyful performance spaces.
items.sourceNote: North America launched in November 2005; Europe follows in 2006.

items.title: Mario Kart DS
items.body: Nintendo makes handheld online racing feel normal for the first time.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for November 2005.

items.title: Resident Evil 4
items.body: Capcom changes the grammar of action horror, with Europe receiving it in March.
items.sourceNote: Nintendo UK lists March 18, 2005 for Europe; North America launched in January.

items.title: Gran Turismo 4
items.body: Polyphony turns car collecting and licence tests into another PS2 ritual.
items.sourceNote: Regional dates vary across February and March 2005.

items.title: God of War
items.body: Sony Santa Monica turns mythic violence into a new PlayStation icon.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 2005 in North America; PAL timing should be checked.

items.title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell: Chaos Theory
items.body: Ubisoft's stealth series reaches one of its sharpest forms.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 2005.

items.title: Jade Empire
items.body: BioWare builds a martial-arts RPG with old Xbox warmth.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for April 2005.

items.title: Guild Wars
items.body: ArenaNet offers online RPG structure without a monthly subscription.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for April 2005.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: Nintendo DS launches in Europe
items.body: The DS reaches UK shops with touch screens, a stylus and a launch line-up that needs explaining at the counter.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot reports March 11, 2005 and UK pricing.

items.title: PSP launches in Europe
items.body: Sony's handheld reaches Europe as a premium media object with a sharp screen and UMD film dreams.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot reports September 1, 2005.

items.title: Xbox 360 launches
items.body: Microsoft starts the HD console generation in North America, Europe and Japan before Sony or Nintendo respond.
items.sourceNote: Microsoft lists November 22 North America and December 2 Europe.

items.title: Xbox Live Marketplace points forward
items.body: Downloads, demos and profiles make the console feel more like a connected platform.
items.sourceNote: Xbox 360 launch context; verify feature-level dates before final copy.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Forums and files
items.body: Guild Wars and World of Warcraft make online RPGs visible to players who had never used the word MMO before.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Services and servers
items.body: Mario Kart DS makes handheld online play feel approachable, even if router setup can be a ritual of its own.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Voice and identity
items.body: Xbox 360 launches with a more coherent online identity, where profiles, demos and friends feel built in.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Everyday connection
items.body: UK players increasingly live between physical shops and online infrastructure: patches, demos, forums, preorders and account names.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like standing between eras. Resident Evil 4 and Shadow of the Colossus suggested the old machines still had magic left, while Xbox 360 promised expensive new weather.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: The DS and PSP argument was wonderfully physical: stylus versus screen, oddness versus gloss, Nintendo toybox versus Sony gadget.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: Guitar Hero made spectators matter again, turning embarrassment into performance.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: In the UK, December 2005 had the charge of a hardware launch without quite the certainty of a revolution; the future was there, but it came bundled, scarce and pricey.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
